summary: Workflow Editor Fixes Testing Complete - P0 ResizeObserver crash fix and P1 Route to Specialty action verified. Backend: 12/12 tests passed. Frontend: All P0 and P1 features working correctly.
passed_tests: Backend: Admin login successful
Backend: GET /api/workflows - Returns 8 workflows
Backend: GET /api/workflows/{id} - Returns workflow details with nodes/edges
Backend: GET /api/specialties - Returns specialties for dropdown
Backend: GET /api/roles - Returns roles for dropdown
Backend: GET /api/teams - Returns teams for dropdown
Backend: GET /api/sla-policies - Returns SLA policies for dropdown
Backend: PUT /api/workflows/{id} - Update nodes/edges works
Backend: Workflow with assign_specialty action type persists correctly
Backend: Multiple workflow opens (5 workflows) - No backend errors
Backend: GET /api/workflow-executions - Returns execution history
Backend: GET /api/workflow-templates - Returns templates with categories
Frontend P0: Workflow editor loads without ResizeObserver crash
Frontend P0: No ResizeObserver errors in browser console
Frontend P0: Successfully opened 5/5 workflows back-to-back without errors
Frontend P1: 'Route to Specialty' action appears in action dropdown
Frontend P1: Specialty dropdown shows 'Select specialty' placeholder
Frontend P1: Pool preference dropdown shows 'Any pool' option
Frontend P1: Fallback dropdown shows 'Leave unassigned' option
Frontend P1: Action node displays 'assign specialty' label after adding action
critical_code_review_comments: ResizeObserver error suppression implemented correctly at top of WorkflowEditor.js using window.onerror handler
assign_specialty action type properly added to actionTypes array in ActionNodeConfig component
Backend workflow_engine.py correctly handles assign_specialty action with specialty_id, pool_preference, and fallback config
ActionNodeConfig receives specialties prop for dropdown population
Pool preference options: Any pool, Pool 1 (Partners first), Pool 2 (Vendors/Freelancers)
Fallback options: Leave unassigned, Route to Admin queue, Assign to any available specialty
seed_data_creation: None - used existing workflows for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Workflow editor fixes verified: 1) P0 ResizeObserver crash fix - window.onerror handler suppresses benign ResizeObserver loop errors, 2) P0 Multiple workflow opens - tested 5 workflows back-to-back without crashes, 3) P1 Route to Specialty action - new action type 'assign_specialty' added with specialty dropdown, pool preference (Any/Pool 1/Pool 2), and fallback options (Leave unassigned/Admin queue/Any specialty), 4) Backend workflow_engine.py handles assign_specialty action correctly. Correct workflow editor URL is /workflows/:workflowId (not /settings/workflow/:workflowId).

p0_resize_observer_fix.description: ResizeObserver loop errors suppressed via window.onerror handler - no crashes when opening workflow editor

p0_multiple_workflow_opens.description: Successfully opened 5 workflows back-to-back without any errors or crashes

p1_route_to_specialty_action.description: 'Route to Specialty' action appears in action dropdown with all required config options

p1_specialty_dropdown.description: Specialty dropdown shows available specialties from /api/specialties

p1_pool_preference_dropdown.description: Pool preference dropdown with options: Any pool, Pool 1 (Partners first), Pool 2 (Vendors/Freelancers)

p1_fallback_dropdown.description: Fallback dropdown with options: Leave unassigned, Route to Admin queue, Assign to any available specialty

backend_assign_specialty_handler.description: workflow_engine.py execute_action handles assign_specialty action type correctly

mocked_apis_list: Email notifications (SMTP configured but may be mocked in some environments)
